message.content:
Read-only scout of a bash codebase. Working dir: /Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_beta_recipemuster. Do NOT edit anything. Report conclusions as `file_path:line_number` plus a one-line explanation each.

Context: We are retiring "enshrine". The docket says onboarding rides this cutover: "flip the enshrine handbook track + RBYC_ENSHRINE + the matching rbtdro_onboarding fixture. The handbook render only asserts exit-0, so stale prose passes silently — this done is the only guard."

Find and report precisely:
1. `RBYC_ENSHRINE` — its definition (a linked-term constant in `Tools/rbk/rbyc_common.sh`) and every place it's used. What text does it carry (the enshrine prose fragment)?
2. The enshrine handbook/onboarding TRACK — the onboarding tabtarget(s) that teach enshrine. Likely candidates: `tt/rbw-Oda.*Airgap*.sh` (enshrine→conjure base→airgap), `tt/rbw-Odf.*FirstBuild*.sh`, `tt/rbw-Odb.*Bind*.sh`. Which onboarding track actually walks the operator through `enshrine` / `rbw-dE`? Find the handbook content source (the `rbho*`/`rbh0` handbook module or yelp fragment that the track renders).
3. `rbtdro_onboarding` — the theurge onboarding fixture (`Tools/rbk/rbtd/rbtdro_onboarding.rs` or similar). What does it assert about the enshrine track? Confirm whether it only checks exit-0 (so stale prose passes silently) or asserts specific content.

Tight conclusions with file:line. I need to know exactly what to flip when enshrine prose becomes bole prose, and what (if anything) actually guards correctness.

BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

170	/// Read an env-file value or None if absent. Mirrors the helpers in rbtdrk
171	/// and rbtdrp — kept local to avoid cross-module coupling.
172	fn rbtdro_read_env_value(path: &Path, key: &str) -> Option<String> {
173	    let content = std::fs::read_to_string(path).ok()?;
174	    let prefix = format!("{}=", key);
175	    for line in content.lines() {
176	        let trimmed = line.trim_start();
177	        if trimmed.starts_with('#') {
178	            continue;
179	        }
180	        if let Some(rest) = trimmed.strip_prefix(&prefix) {
181	            return Some(rest.to_string());
182	        }
183	    }
184	    None
185	}

228	fn rbtdro_invoke_logged(
229	    ctx: &mut rbtdri_Context,
230	    colophon: &str,
231	    args: &[&str],
232	    extra_env: &[(&str, &str)],
233	    dir: &Path,
234	    label: &str,
235	) -> Result<rbtdri_InvokeResult, String> {
236	    let result = rbtdri_invoke_global(ctx, colophon, args, extra_env)?;
237	    let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join(format!("{}-stdout.txt", label)), &result.stdout);
238	    let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join(format!("{}-stderr.txt", label)), &result.stderr);

242	/// Invoke a tabtarget via `rbtdro_invoke_logged` and convert non-success outcomes
243	/// into Fail verdicts with consistent operation-prefixed messages. `target` is
244	/// the per-call distinguishing string (vessel sigil, vessel dir, nameplate)
245	/// included in the error prefix; pass `""` for operations without a target.
246	fn rbtdro_invoke_or_fail(
247	    ctx: &mut rbtdri_Context,
248	    operation: &str,
249	    target: &str,
250	    colophon: &str,
251	    args: &[&str],
252	    extra_env: &[(&str, &str)],
253	    dir: &Path,
254	    label: &str,
255	) -> Result<rbtdri_InvokeResult, rbtdre_Verdict> {
256	    let prefix = if target.is_empty() {
257	        operation.to_string()
258	    } else {
259	        format!("{} {}", operation, target)
260	    };
261	    let result = rbtdro_invoke_logged(ctx, colophon, args, extra_env, dir, label)
262	        .map_err(|e| rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("{} invocation: {}", prefix, e)))?;
263	    if result.exit_code != 0 {
264	        return Err(rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!(
265	            "{} exit {}\n{}",
266	            prefix, result.exit_code, result.stderr
267	        )));
268	    }
269	    Ok(result)
270	}

390	/// is locally resolvable before the BURE_TWEAK-overridden ordain.

407	/// Write a value into a vessel's rbrv.env. Same atomic-rename pattern as
408	/// rbtdro_drive_hallmark, but targeting the vessel's regime file rather than
409	/// a nameplate's. Returns Err if the variable line is not found.
410	fn rbtdro_write_vessel_env(
411	    root: &Path,
412	    vessel_dir: &str,
413	    var_name: &str,
414	    value: &str,
415	) -> Result<(), String> {
416	    let rbrv_path = root.join(vessel_dir).join(RBTDRO_VESSEL_RBRV_FILE);
417	    let file = std::fs::File::open(&rbrv_path)
418	        .map_err(|e| format!("open rbrv.env for {}: {}", vessel_dir, e))?;
419	
420	    let lines: Vec<String> = BufReader::new(file)
421	        .lines()
422	        .collect::<Result<_, _>>()
423	        .map_err(|e| format!("read rbrv.env for {}: {}", vessel_dir, e))?;
424	
425	    let prefix = format!("{}=", var_name);
426	    let mut found = false;
427	    let rewritten: Vec<String> = lines
428	        .into_iter()
429	        .map(|line| {
430	            if line.starts_with(&prefix) {
431	                found = true;
432	                format!("{}={}", var_name, value)
433	            } else {
434	                line
435	            }
436	        })
437	        .collect();
438	
439	    if !found {
440	        return Err(format!(
441	            "variable {} not found in {}/rbrv.env",
442	            var_name, vessel_dir
443	        ));
444	    }
445	
446	    let tmp_path = rbrv_path.with_extension("env.write_tmp");
447	    {
448	        let mut tmp = std::fs::File::create(&tmp_path)
449	            .map_err(|e| format!("create tmp rbrv.env for {}: {}", vessel_dir, e))?;
450	        for line in &rewritten {
451	            writeln!(tmp, "{}", line)
452	                .map_err(|e| format!("write tmp rbrv.env for {}: {}", vessel_dir, e))?;
453	        }
454	    }
455	    std::fs::rename(&tmp_path, &rbrv_path)
456	        .map_err(|e| format!("atomic replace rbrv.env for {}: {}", vessel_dir, e))?;
457	    Ok(())
458	}

529	/// Enumerate every vessel's rbrv.env under the vessels directory — the dynamic
530	/// set the wildcard yoke rewrites. Repo-relative paths, one per vessel subdir
531	/// that carries an rbrv.env, sorted for stable commit staging.
532	fn rbtdro_all_vessel_rbrv_paths(root: &Path) -> Result<Vec<String>, String> {
533	    let vessels_rel = crate::rbtd_vessels_dir!();
534	    let vessels_abs = root.join(vessels_rel);
535	    let mut paths: Vec<String> = Vec::new();
536	    let entries = std::fs::read_dir(&vessels_abs)
537	        .map_err(|e| format!("read vessels dir {}: {}", vessels_abs.display(), e))?;
538	    for entry in entries {
539	        let entry = entry.map_err(|e| format!("read vessels dir entry: {}", e))?;
540	        if entry.path().is_dir() {
541	            let name = entry.file_name();
542	            let rbrv_rel = format!("{}/{}/{}", vessels_rel, name.to_string_lossy(), RBTDRO_VESSEL_RBRV_FILE);
543	            if root.join(&rbrv_rel).is_file() {
544	                paths.push(rbrv_rel);
545	            }
546	        }
547	    }
548	    paths.sort();
549	    Ok(paths)
550	}

552	/// Surgical auto-commit helper. Each caller passes the explicit repo-relative
553	/// files its step changed (was `git add -A`, which once swept an unrelated
554	/// working-tree edit into a hallmark commit). Checks `git status --porcelain`
555	/// scoped to just those files first; an empty result means the step changed
556	/// nothing (e.g. an idempotent yoke, or terminal graft with no consumers) and
557	/// is a clean no-op, not an error. Otherwise `git add -- <files>` then
558	/// `git commit -m <message>`. Theurge launches from project root so no explicit
559	/// current_dir is needed.
560	fn rbtdro_git_commit(files: &[String], message: &str) -> Result<(), rbtdre_Verdict> {
561	    let mut status_args: Vec<&str> = vec!["status", "--porcelain", "--"];
562	    status_args.extend(files.iter().map(String::as_str));
563	    let status = std::process::Command::new("git")
564	        .args(&status_args)
565	        .output()
566	        .map_err(|e| {
567	            rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("git status --porcelain exec failed: {}", e))
568	        })?;
569	    if !status.status.success() {
570	        return Err(rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!(
571	            "git status --porcelain exited {}",
572	            status.status.code().unwrap_or(-1)
573	        )));
574	    }
575	    if String::from_utf8_lossy(&status.stdout).trim().is_empty() {
576	        // None of the owned files changed — clean no-op, not an error.
577	        return Ok(());
578	    }
579	
580	    let mut add_args: Vec<&str> = vec!["add", "--"];
581	    add_args.extend(files.iter().map(String::as_str));
582	    let add = std::process::Command::new("git")
583	        .args(&add_args)
584	        .output()
585	        .map_err(|e| rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("git add exec failed: {}", e)))?;
586	    if !add.status.success() {
587	        return Err(rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!(
588	            "git add exited {}: {}",
589	            add.status.code().unwrap_or(-1),
590	            String::from_utf8_lossy(&add.stderr).trim()
591	        )));
592	    }
593	
594	    let commit = std::process::Command::new("git")
595	        .args(["commit", "-m", message])
596	        .output()
597	        .map_err(|e| rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("git commit exec failed: {}", e)))?;
598	    if !commit.status.success() {
599	        return Err(rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!(
600	            "git commit exited {}: {}",
601	            commit.status.code().unwrap_or(-1),
602	            String::from_utf8_lossy(&commit.stderr).trim()
603	        )));
604	    }
605	    Ok(())
606	}

659	/// Inscribe the depot-wide reliquary toolchain. Captures the reliquary
660	/// stamp from BURV fact, persists it to the fixture scratch file, then
661	/// yokes the stamp into all ordain-side vessels in one pass and auto-commits.
662	fn rbtdro_onboarding_inscribe_reliquary(dir: &Path) -> rbtdre_Verdict {
663	    let probe = rbtdrb_Probe {
664	        name: "governor RBRA present",
665	        check: rbtdro_probe_governor_rbra,
666	        remediation: "rerun canonical-establish (rbtdrk_governor_mantle) before this fixture",
667	    };
668	    if let Err(v) = rbtdrb_assert(&probe) {
669	        return v;
670	    }
671	    rbtdrc_with_ctx(|ctx| rbtdro_onboarding_inscribe_reliquary_impl(ctx, dir))
672	}
673	
674	fn rbtdro_onboarding_inscribe_reliquary_impl(
675	    ctx: &mut rbtdri_Context,
676	    dir: &Path,
677	) -> rbtdre_Verdict {
678	    let root = ctx.project_root().to_path_buf();
679	    let result = match rbtdro_invoke_or_fail(
680	        ctx,
681	        RBTDGC_VERB_INSCRIBE,
682	        "",
683	        RBTDGC_INSCRIBE_RELIQUARY,
684	        &[],
685	        &[],
686	        dir,
687	        RBTDGC_VERB_INSCRIBE,
688	    ) {
689	        Ok(r) => r,
690	        Err(v) => return v,
691	    };
692	
693	    let stamp = match rbtdri_read_burv_fact(&result, RBTDRC_FACT_RELIQUARY) {
694	        Ok(s) => s,
695	        Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("read reliquary fact: {}", e)),
696	    };
697	    let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join("reliquary-stamp.txt"), &stamp);
698	
699	    // Wildcard-yoke: single invocation writes RBRV_RELIQUARY into every
700	    // vessel under ${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}.
701	    if let Err(v) = rbtdro_yoke(ctx, dir, &stamp, RBTDGC_VERB_YOKE) {
702	        return v;
703	    }
704	
705	    // Commit the rbrv.env changes for all yoked vessels — the wildcard yoke
706	    // rewrites every vessel's rbrv.env, so stage that enumerated set.
707	    let yoked = match rbtdro_all_vessel_rbrv_paths(&root) {
708	        Ok(p) => p,
709	        Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("enumerate vessel rbrv.env set: {}", e)),
710	    };
711	    if let Err(v) = rbtdro_git_commit(
712	        &yoked,
713	        &format!(
714	            "inscribe-reliquary: {} stamp across all vessels",
715	            RBTDGC_VERB_YOKE
716	        ),
717	    ) {
718	        return v;
719	    }
720	
721	    rbtdre_Verdict::Pass
722	}

815	
816	    // Verification tail: wrest, summon, plumb_full, rekon — exercises the
817	    // hallmark supply chain end-to-end. Abjure is omitted because downstream
818	    // crucible fixtures consume this hallmark.
819	    let wrest_locator = format!(
820	        "{}/{}/{}:{}",

822	    );
823	    if let Err(v) = rbtdro_invoke_or_fail(

825	        "wrest",
826	        &wrest_locator,
827	        RBTDGC_WREST_HALLMARK_IMAGE,
828	        &[&wrest_locator],
829	        &[],
830	        dir,
831	        "wrest-conjure",
832	    ) {
833	        return v;
834	    }
835	
836	    let image_ref = format!("{}/{}/{}:{}", gar_root, ark_stem, RBTDRC_ARK_BASENAME_IMAGE, hallmark);
837	    let about_ref = format!("{}/{}/{}:{}", gar_root, ark_stem, RBTDRC_ARK_BASENAME_ABOUT, hallmark);
838	    let vouch_ref = format!("{}/{}/{}:{}", gar_root, ark_stem, RBTDRC_ARK_BASENAME_VOUCH, hallmark);
839	
840	    if let Err(v) = rbtdro_invoke_or_fail(
841	        ctx,
842	        "summon",

845	        &[&hallmark],
846	        &[],
847	        dir,
848	        "summon-conjure",
849	    ) {
850	        return v;
851	    }
852	    for ark_ref in [&image_ref, &about_ref, &vouch_ref] {
853	        if !rbtdrc_docker_inspect(ark_ref) {
854	            return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!(
855	                "summon: ark not local after pull: {}",
856	                ark_ref
857	            ));
858	        }
859	    }
860	
861	    let plumb_full = match rbtdro_invoke_or_fail(
862	        ctx,
863	        "plumb_full",
864	        &hallmark,
865	        RBTDGC_PLUMB_FULL,
866	        &[&hallmark],
867	        &[],
868	        dir,
869	        "plumb-full-conjure",
870	    ) {
871	        Ok(r) => r,
872	        Err(v) => return v,
873	    };
874	    for marker in ["SLSA", "SBOM", "Dockerfile"] {
875	        if !plumb_full.stdout.contains(marker) {
876	            return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!(
877	                "plumb_full: marker '{}' not in stdout",
878	                marker
879	            ));
880	        }
881	    }

883	    let rekon = match rbtdro_invoke_or_fail(
884	        ctx,
885	        "rekon",
886	        &hallmark,
887	        RBTDGC_REKON_HALLMARK,
888	        &[&hallmark],
889	        &[],
890	        dir,
891	        "rekon-conjure",
892	    ) {
893	        Ok(r) => r,
894	        Err(v) => return v,
895	    };
896	    for basename in [
897	        RBTDRC_ARK_BASENAME_IMAGE,
898	        RBTDRC_ARK_BASENAME_ABOUT,
899	        RBTDRC_ARK_BASENAME_VOUCH,
900	        RBTDRC_ARK_BASENAME_ATTEST,
901	    ] {

904	                "rekon: basename '{}' not marked yes\nstdout:\n{}",

907	        }
908	    }
909	
910	    for nameplate in RBTDRO_CONSUMERS_SENTRY_TETHER {
911	        if let Err(e) =
912	            rbtdro_drive_hallmark(&root, nameplate, RBTDRO_HALLMARK_VAR_SENTRY, &hallmark)

924	        .collect();

926	        &owned,
927	        "ordain-conjure: sentry-tether hallmark + propagate to consumers",
928	    ) {

995	        name: "reliquary stamp captured",
996	        check: rbtdro_probe_reliquary_stamp,
997	        remediation: "rerun rbtdro_onboarding_inscribe_reliquary before this case",
998	    };
999	    if let Err(v) = rbtdrb_assert(&probe) {

1005	fn rbtdro_onboarding_ordain_airgap_chain_impl(ctx: &mut rbtdri_Context, dir: &Path) -> rbtdre_Verdict {
1006	    let root = ctx.project_root().to_path_buf();
1007	
1008	    let forge_sigil = RBTDRO_VESSEL_DIR_AIRGAP_FORGE
1009	        .rsplit('/')
1010	        .next()
1011	        .unwrap_or(RBTDRO_VESSEL_DIR_AIRGAP_FORGE);
1012	
1013	    if let Err(v) = rbtdro_enshrine(ctx, dir, forge_sigil, "enshrine-upstream") {
1014	        return v;
1015	    }
1016	    // Commit before ordain-forge: ordain has clean-tree precondition. Enshrine
1017	    // wrote the forge vessel's RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR digests.
1018	    if let Err(v) = rbtdro_git_commit(
1019	        &[rbtdro_vessel_rbrv_path(RBTDRO_VESSEL_DIR_AIRGAP_FORGE)],
1020	        "ordain-airgap: enshrine upstream rust base into forge vessel",
1021	    ) {
1022	        return v;
1023	    }
1024	
1025	    // Capture the forge hallmark — it becomes the airgap-bottle's base via
1026	    // a hallmark-namespace locator (mechanism (c)).
1027	    let forge_hallmark = match rbtdro_ordain_capture(
1028	        ctx,
1029	        dir,
1030	        RBTDRO_VESSEL_DIR_AIRGAP_FORGE,
1031	        &[],
1032	        "ordain-forge",
1033	    ) {
1034	        Ok(h) => h,
1035	        Err(v) => return v,
1036	    };
1037	
1038	    // Write the hallmark-base locator into airgap-bottle's rbrv.env. The slot
1039	    // points at the forge image inside its hallmark subtree; conjure resolves
1040	    // this locator to a full GAR ref at airgap-bottle build time.
1041	    let airgap_anchor = format!(
1042	        "{}/{}/{}:{}",
1043	        RBTDRC_GAR_CATEGORY_HALLMARKS,
1044	        forge_hallmark.trim(),
1045	        RBTDRC_ARK_BASENAME_IMAGE,
1046	        forge_hallmark.trim(),
1047	    );
1048	    if let Err(e) = rbtdro_write_vessel_env(
1049	        &root,
1050	        RBTDRO_VESSEL_DIR_AIRGAP_BOTTLE,
1051	        RBTDRO_AIRGAP_BASE_ANCHOR_VAR,
1052	        &airgap_anchor,
1053	    ) {
1054	        return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("write airgap-bottle anchor: {}", e));
1055	    }
1056	    let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join("airgap-anchor.txt"), &airgap_anchor);
1057	
1058	    // Commit the locator write before ordain-airgap: ordain has clean-tree
1059	    // precondition. The forge hallmark's existence in GAR is established by
1060	    // ordain-forge's success above — no separate enshrine validation step.
1061	    if let Err(v) = rbtdro_git_commit(
1062	        &[rbtdro_vessel_rbrv_path(RBTDRO_VESSEL_DIR_AIRGAP_BOTTLE)],
1063	        "ordain-airgap: write forge-hallmark locator into airgap-bottle base anchor",
1064	    ) {
1065	        return v;
1066	    }
1067	
1068	    let airgap_hallmark = match rbtdro_ordain_capture(
1069	        ctx,
1070	        dir,
1071	        RBTDRO_VESSEL_DIR_AIRGAP_BOTTLE,
1072	        &[],
1073	        "ordain-airgap",
1074	    ) {
1075	        Ok(h) => h,
1076	        Err(v) => return v,
1077	    };
1078	
1079	    for nameplate in RBTDRO_CONSUMERS_AIRGAP_BOTTLE {
1080	        if let Err(e) =
1081	            rbtdro_drive_hallmark(&root, nameplate, RBTDRO_HALLMARK_VAR_BOTTLE, &airgap_hallmark)
1082	        {
1083	            return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!(
1084	                "drive airgap hallmark into {}: {}",
1085	                nameplate, e
1086	            ));
1087	        }
1088	    }
1089	
1090	    let owned: Vec<String> = RBTDRO_CONSUMERS_AIRGAP_BOTTLE
1091	        .iter()
1092	        .map(|n| rbtdro_nameplate_rbrn_path(n))
1093	        .collect();
1094	    if let Err(v) =
1095	        rbtdro_git_commit(&owned, "ordain-airgap: airgap-bottle hallmark + propagate to moriah")
1096	    {
1097	        return v;
1098	    }
1099	
1100	    rbtdre_Verdict::Pass
1101	}
1102	
1103	/// Pin upstream PlantUML by digest. Bind mode reads RBRV_BIND_IMAGE from
1104	/// rbev-bottle-plantuml/rbrv.env and mirrors the digest into GAR via Cloud
1105	/// Build (skopeo from reliquary + about/vouch metadata). Propagates plantuml
1106	/// hallmark to pluml via RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK.
1107	fn rbtdro_onboarding_ordain_bind_plantuml(dir: &Path) -> rbtdre_Verdict {
1108	    let probe = rbtdrb_Probe {
1109	        name: "reliquary stamp captured",
1110	        check: rbtdro_probe_reliquary_stamp,
1111	        remediation: "rerun rbtdro_onboarding_inscribe_reliquary before this case",
1112	    };
1113	    if let Err(v) = rbtdrb_assert(&probe) {
1114	        return v;
1115	    }
1116	    rbtdrc_with_ctx(|ctx| rbtdro_onboarding_ordain_bind_plantuml_impl(ctx, dir))
1117	}
1118	
1119	fn rbtdro_onboarding_ordain_bind_plantuml_impl(ctx: &mut rbtdri_Context, dir: &Path) -> rbtdre_Verdict {
1120	    let root = ctx.project_root().to_path_buf();
1121	
1122	    let hallmark = match rbtdro_ordain_capture(
1123	        ctx,
1124	        dir,
1125	        RBTDRO_VESSEL_DIR_PLANTUML,
1126	        &[],
1127	        "ordain-bind",
1128	    ) {
1129	        Ok(h) => h,
1130	        Err(v) => return v,
1131	    };
1132	
1133	    // Verification tail: wrest, plumb_compact, surgical pouch jettison.
1134	    // Pouch jettison + post-rekon proves selective ark deletion preserves
1135	    // image (load-bearing for downstream pluml, which needs only the image).
1136	    // Abjure is omitted because pluml consumes this hallmark.
1137	    let wrest_locator = format!(
1138	        "{}/{}/{}:{}",
1139	        RBTDRC_GAR_CATEGORY_HALLMARKS, hallmark, RBTDRC_ARK_BASENAME_IMAGE, hallmark
1140	    );
1141	    if let Err(v) = rbtdro_invoke_or_fail(
1142	        ctx,
1143	        "wrest",
1144	        &wrest_locator,
1145	        RBTDGC_WREST_HALLMARK_IMAGE,
1146	        &[&wrest_locator],
1147	        &[],
1148	        dir,
1149	        "wrest-bind",
1150	    ) {
1151	        return v;
1152	    }
1153	
1154	    let plumb_compact = match rbtdro_invoke_or_fail(
1155	        ctx,
1156	        "plumb_compact",
1157	        &hallmark,
1158	        RBTDGC_PLUMB_COMPACT,
1159	        &[&hallmark],
1160	        &[],
1161	        dir,
1162	        "plumb-compact-bind",
1163	    ) {
1164	        Ok(r) => r,
1165	        Err(v) => return v,
1166	    };
1167	    if !plumb_compact.stdout.contains(&hallmark) {
1168	        return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!(
1169	            "plumb_compact: hallmark '{}' not in stdout",
1170	            hallmark
1171	        ));
1172	    }
1173	    if !plumb_compact.stdout.contains("HALLMARK PLUMB:") {
1174	        return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(
1175	            "plumb_compact: expected 'HALLMARK PLUMB:' marker in stdout".to_string(),
1176	        );
1177	    }
1178	
1179	    let jettison_locator = format!(
1180	        "{}/{}/{}:{}",
1181	        RBTDRC_GAR_CATEGORY_HALLMARKS, hallmark, RBTDRC_ARK_BASENAME_POUCH, hallmark
1182	    );
1183	    if let Err(v) = rbtdro_invoke_or_fail(
1184	        ctx,
1185	        "jettison",
1186	        &jettison_locator,
1187	        RBTDGC_JETTISON_HALLMARK_IMAGE,
1188	        &[&jettison_locator],
1189	        &[(RBTDRI_BURE_CONFIRM_KEY, RBTDRI_BURE_CONFIRM_SKIP)],
1190	        dir,
1191	        "jettison-pouch-bind",
1192	    ) {
1193	        return v;
1194	    }
1195	
1196	    let rekon_after = match rbtdro_invoke_or_fail(
1197	        ctx,
1198	        "rekon",
1199	        &hallmark,
1200	        RBTDGC_REKON_HALLMARK,
1201	        &[&hallmark],
1202	        &[],
1203	        dir,
1204	        "rekon-after-jettison-bind",
1205	    ) {
1206	        Ok(r) => r,
1207	        Err(v) => return v,
1208	    };
1209	    if rbtdrc_rekon_basename_yes(&rekon_after.stdout, RBTDRC_ARK_BASENAME_POUCH) {
1210	        return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!(
1211	            "rekon: pouch still present after jettison\nstdout:\n{}",
1212	            rekon_after.stdout
1213	        ));
1214	    }
1215	    if !rbtdrc_rekon_basename_yes(&rekon_after.stdout, RBTDRC_ARK_BASENAME_IMAGE) {
1216	        return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!(
1217	            "rekon: image disappeared after pouch jettison (collateral damage)\nstdout:\n{}",
1218	            rekon_after.stdout
1219	        ));
1220	    }
1221	
1222	    for nameplate in RBTDRO_CONSUMERS_PLANTUML_BOTTLE {
1223	        if let Err(e) =
1224	            rbtdro_drive_hallmark(&root, nameplate, RBTDRO_HALLMARK_VAR_BOTTLE, &hallmark)
1225	        {
1226	            return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!(
1227	                "drive plantuml hallmark into {}: {}",
1228	                nameplate, e
1229	            ));
1230	        }
1231	    }
1232	
1233	    let owned: Vec<String> = RBTDRO_CONSUMERS_PLANTUML_BOTTLE
1234	        .iter()
1235	        .map(|n| rbtdro_nameplate_rbrn_path(n))
1236	        .collect();
1237	    if let Err(v) =
1238	        rbtdro_git_commit(&owned, "ordain-bind: plantuml-bottle hallmark + propagate to pluml")
1239	    {
1240	        return v;
1241	    }
1242	
1243	    rbtdre_Verdict::Pass
1244	}
1245	
1246	/// Pull the graft source image, then ordain rbev-graft-demo with a
1247	/// BURE_TWEAK overriding RBRV_GRAFT_IMAGE in-process. No consumers —
1248	/// graft-demo is terminal.
1249	fn rbtdro_onboarding_ordain_graft_demo(dir: &Path) -> rbtdre_Verdict {
1250	    let probe = rbtdrb_Probe {
1251	        name: "reliquary stamp captured",
1252	        check: rbtdro_probe_reliquary_stamp,
1253	        remediation: "rerun rbtdro_onboarding_inscribe_reliquary before this case",
1254	    };
1255	    if let Err(v) = rbtdrb_assert(&probe) {
1256	        return v;
1257	    }
1258	    rbtdrc_with_ctx(|ctx| rbtdro_onboarding_ordain_graft_demo_impl(ctx, dir))
1259	}
1260	
1261	fn rbtdro_onboarding_ordain_graft_demo_impl(ctx: &mut rbtdri_Context, dir: &Path) -> rbtdre_Verdict {
1262	    if let Err(e) = rbtdro_docker_pull(RBTDRO_GRAFT_SOURCE_IMAGE) {
1263	        return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!(
1264	            "docker pull {}: {}",
1265	            RBTDRO_GRAFT_SOURCE_IMAGE, e
1266	        ));
1267	    }
1268	
1269	    // Capture source layer DiffIDs before ordain — graft pushes bytes through
1270	    // GAR's manifest-envelope normalization; round-trip identity proves the
1271	    // bytes survived unchanged.
1272	    let source_layers = match rbtdrc_docker_layers_capture(RBTDRO_GRAFT_SOURCE_IMAGE) {
1273	        Ok(v) => v,
1274	        Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("source image inspect: {}", e)),
1275	    };
1276	    let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join("source-layers.txt"), &source_layers);
1277	
1278	    let extra_env: &[(&str, &str)] = &[
1279	        ("BURE_TWEAK_NAME", RBTDRO_GRAFT_TWEAK_NAME),
1280	        ("BURE_TWEAK_VALUE", RBTDRO_GRAFT_SOURCE_IMAGE),
1281	    ];
1282	    let (hallmark, gar_root, ark_stem) = match rbtdro_ordain_capture_full(
1283	        ctx,
1284	        dir,
1285	        RBTDRO_VESSEL_DIR_GRAFT,
1286	        extra_env,
1287	        "ordain-graft",
1288	    ) {
1289	        Ok(facts) => facts,
1290	        Err(v) => return v,
1291	    };
1292	
1293	    // Verification tail: wrest, layer-DiffID round-trip, abjure. graft-demo
1294	    // is terminal (no consumers), so abjure is safe.
1295	    let wrest_locator = format!(
1296	        "{}/{}/{}:{}",
1297	        RBTDRC_GAR_CATEGORY_HALLMARKS, hallmark, RBTDRC_ARK_BASENAME_IMAGE, hallmark
1298	    );
1299	    if let Err(v) = rbtdro_invoke_or_fail(
1300	        ctx,
1301	        "wrest",
1302	        &wrest_locator,
1303	        RBTDGC_WREST_HALLMARK_IMAGE,
1304	        &[&wrest_locator],
1305	        &[],
1306	        dir,
1307	        "wrest-graft",
1308	    ) {
1309	        return v;
1310	    }
1311	
1312	    let image_ref = format!("{}/{}/{}:{}", gar_root, ark_stem, RBTDRC_ARK_BASENAME_IMAGE, hallmark);
1313	    let wrested_layers = match rbtdrc_docker_layers_capture(&image_ref) {
1314	        Ok(v) => v,
1315	        Err(e) => return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("wrested image inspect: {}", e)),
1316	    };
1317	    let _ = std::fs::write(dir.join("wrested-layers.txt"), &wrested_layers);
1318	    if source_layers != wrested_layers {
1319	        return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!(
1320	            "graft round-trip layer mismatch:\n  source:  {}\n  wrested: {}",
1321	            source_layers, wrested_layers
1322	        ));
1323	    }
1324	
1325	    if let Err(e) = rbtdrc_docker_rmi(&[&image_ref]) {
1326	        return rbtdre_Verdict::Fail(format!("rmi: {}", e));
1327	    }
1328	
1329	    // graft-demo is terminal (no consumers); the step writes no regime file, so
1330	    // this commit owns only the graft vessel's rbrv.env and is normally a clean
1331	    // no-op (the scoped status check skips it when nothing changed).
1332	    if let Err(v) = rbtdro_git_commit(
1333	        &[rbtdro_vessel_rbrv_path(RBTDRO_VESSEL_DIR_GRAFT)],
1334	        "ordain-graft: graft-demo hallmark",
1335	    ) {
1336	        return v;
1337	    }
1338	
1339	    if let Err(v) = rbtdro_invoke_or_fail(
1340	        ctx,
1341	        "abjure",
1342	        &hallmark,
1343	        RBTDGC_ABJURE_HALLMARK,
1344	        &[&hallmark],
1345	        &[(RBTDRI_BURE_CONFIRM_KEY, RBTDRI_BURE_CONFIRM_SKIP)],
1346	        dir,
1347	        "abjure-graft",
1348	    ) {
1349	        return v;
1350	    }
1351	
1352	    rbtdre_Verdict::Pass
1353	}
1354	
1355	// ── Case registry ────────────────────────────────────────────
1356	
1357	pub static RBTDRO_CASES_ONBOARDING_SEQUENCE: &[rbtdre_Case] = &[
1358	    case!(rbtdro_onboarding_inscribe_reliquary),
1359	    case!(rbtdro_onboarding_kludge_tadmor),
1360	    case!(rbtdro_onboarding_kludge_ccyolo),
1361	    case!(rbtdro_onboarding_ordain_conjure_sentry),
1362	    case!(rbtdro_onboarding_ordain_conjure_jupyter),
1363	    case!(rbtdro_onboarding_ordain_airgap_chain),
1364	    case!(rbtdro_onboarding_ordain_bind_plantuml),
1365	    case!(rbtdro_onboarding_ordain_graft_demo),
1366	];
1367	
1368	pub static RBTDRO_FIXTURE_ONBOARDING_SEQUENCE: rbtdre_Fixture = rbtdre_Fixture {
1369	    name: RBTDRM_FIXTURE_ONBOARDING_SEQUENCE,
1370	    disposition: rbtdre_Disposition::StateProgressing,
1371	    setup: None,
1372	    teardown: None,
1373	    cases: RBTDRO_CASES_ONBOARDING_SEQUENCE,
1374	};

44	  local -r z_tether_rbrn="${RBCC_moorings_dir}/${z_tether_moniker}/${RBCC_rbrn_file}"
45	
46	  local z_has_director=0
47	  local z_secrets_dir=""

53	    z_has_director=1
54	  fi

56	  local z_has_depot=0

88	    esac
89	  fi
90	
91	  buyy_link_yawp "${RBRR_PUBLIC_DOCS_URL}" "Vessel"    "${z_forge_vessel}";   local -r z_lk_forge="${z_buym_yelp}"
92	  buyy_link_yawp "${RBRR_PUBLIC_DOCS_URL}" "Vessel"    "${z_airgap_vessel}";  local -r z_lk_airgap="${z_buym_yelp}"

94	  buyy_link_yawp "${RBRR_PUBLIC_DOCS_URL}" "Nameplate" "${z_tether_moniker}"; local -r z_lk_tadmor="${z_buym_yelp}"
95

134	  buh_line "one ${RBYC_VESSEL} on the ${RBYC_TETHERED} pool."

19	# Recipe Bottle Handbook Onboarding - Start Here (probe-aware menu)

21	set -euo pipefail
22	
23	test -z "${ZRBHO0_SOURCED:-}" || return 0
24	ZRBHO0_SOURCED=1
25	
26	# rbho_start_here — probe-aware menu into the handbook family.
27	# Probes are deliberately narrow: just RBRR populated + any-credential-
28	# present. Highlighting only arrows foundation actions based on repo
29	# state; it does not infer intent from detected roles.
30	rbho_start_here() {
31	  zrbho_sentinel
32	
33	  buc_doc_brief "Probe-aware menu — route learner into the handbook track that fits their state"
34	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
35	
36	  local -r z_docs="${RBRR_PUBLIC_DOCS_URL}"
37	  buyy_link_yawp "${z_docs}" "ccyolo"; local -r z_ccyolo="${z_buym_yelp}"
38	  buyy_link_yawp "${z_docs}" "tadmor"; local -r z_tadmor="${z_buym_yelp}"
39	
40	  buh_section "Recipe Bottle — Onboarding Start"
41	  buh_e
42	  buh_line "  ${RBYC_RECIPE_BOTTLE} builds container images with supply-chain provenance"

content.text: Excellent! Now let me get the exact Enshrine definition from README.md and verify the test assertions in rbtdro_onboarding.rs:

content.content: - **[Tethered](#Tethered)** — Build egress mode allowing public internet access during Cloud Build. [Tethered](#Tethered) builds pull base images from upstream registries at build time — simpler to set up, but dependent on upstream availability. - **[Airgap](#Airgap)** — Build egress mode with no public internet access during Cloud Build. [Airgap](#Airgap) builds draw all dependencies from [Enshrined](#Enshrine) images in the [Depot's](#Depot) registry — fully self-contained, independent of upstream availability. Requires [Enshrining](#Enshrine) base images before the first build. See [Build Isolation](#BuildIsolation) for the security rationale behind these profiles. -- The [Payor](#Payor) then [Mantles](#Mantle) a [Governor](#Governor) service account to administer the [Depot](#Depot). Before the first build can run, the [Depot](#Depot) needs its supply-chain infrastructure in place: upstream base images must be [Enshrined](#Enshrine) into the registry, and a [Reliquary](#Reliquary) of builder tool images must be inscribed.

1005	fn rbtdro_onboarding_ordain_airgap_chain_impl(ctx: &mut rbtdri_Context, dir: &Path) -> rbtdre_Verdict {
1006	    let root = ctx.project_root().to_path_buf();
1007	
1008	    let forge_sigil = RBTDRO_VESSEL_DIR_AIRGAP_FORGE
1009	        .rsplit('/')
1010	        .next()
1011	        .unwrap_or(RBTDRO_VESSEL_DIR_AIRGAP_FORGE);
1012	
1013	    if let Err(v) = rbtdro_enshrine(ctx, dir, forge_sigil, "enshrine-upstream") {
1014	        return v;
1015	    }
1016	    // Commit before ordain-forge: ordain has clean-tree precondition. Enshrine
1017	    // wrote the forge vessel's RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR digests.
1018	    if let Err(v) = rbtdro_git_commit(
1019	        &[rbtdro_vessel_rbrv_path(RBTDRO_VESSEL_DIR_AIRGAP_FORGE)],
1020	        "ordain-airgap: enshrine upstream rust base into forge vessel",
1021	    ) {
1022	        return v;
1023	    }
1024	
1025	    // Capture the forge hallmark — it becomes the airgap-bottle's base via
1026	    // a hallmark-namespace locator (mechanism (c)).
1027	    let forge_hallmark = match rbtdro_ordain_capture(
1028	        ctx,
1029	        dir,
1030	        RBTDRO_VESSEL_DIR_AIRGAP_FORGE,
1031	        &[],
1032	        "ordain-forge",
